intro: NATS exposes a free tier while Lob starts at $260/mo. NATS is positioned for apps sending transactional notifications at scale, while Lob leans toward transactional emails (receipts, alerts). In practice, NATS — Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in message queue. Lob — Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) tool for developers. Specializes in Direct Mail API. NATS carries low lock-in (CNCF open source messaging, portable) while Lob is medium (Direct mail API). NATS rates 5/5 on transparency versus 4/5 for Lob. Listed strengths unique to NATS include multi-channel delivery (email, sms, voice) from one api, while Lob lists transactional emails (receipts, alerts) on its side. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including transactional emails (receipts, alerts). The honest trade-off: NATS is not built for small projects with minimal communication needs, and Lob is not the right call for internal projects, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: NATS is for apps sending transactional notifications at scale; Lob is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose NATS if apps sending transactional notifications at scale and you need a free tier to validate fit.
verdict_b: Choose Lob if transactional emails (receipts, alerts) and the $260/mo entry price fits your budget.

faq.a: NATS has a free tier and Lob starts at $260/mo, so at the lowest end NATS is effectively cheaper. Once you exceed the free limits, the comparison depends on per-unit rates not published here. Price against your actual send or usage volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from NATS to Lob?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving NATS is relatively light thanks to its low lock-in, and onboarding to Lob involves medium lock-in (Direct mail API). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, NATS or Lob?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, NATS rates 5/5 while Lob rates 4/5, giving NATS the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is Lob a good alternative to NATS?
faq.a: Lob is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is transactional emails (receipts, alerts), while NATS's is apps sending transactional notifications at scale. If your job matches NATS's profile more closely, switching to Lob is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: Lob is explicitly not for internal projects.
